back_translation: Elisha the prophet summoned one of the sons of the prophets and said to him, “Gird your waist, take this flask of oil in your hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead.
When you arrive, look for Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi; go in, take him away from his companions, and bring him into an inner room.
Then take the flask of oil, pour it on his head, and say, ‘Thus says the LORD: I anoint you king over Israel.’ Then open the door and flee; do not wait.”
So the young prophet went to Ramoth-gilead.
When he arrived, the army commanders were sitting there. He said, “Commander, I have a message for you.” Jehu asked, “For which of us?” He said, “For you, commander.”
So Jehu got up and went into the house, and the young prophet poured the oil on his head and said, “Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: ‘I anoint you king over Israel, the people of the LORD.
You shall strike down the house of Ahab your master, so that I may avenge the blood of My servants the prophets and the blood of all the servants of the LORD whom Jezebel killed.
The whole house of Ahab shall perish, and I will cut off from Ahab every male in Israel, both slave and free.
I will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat and the house of Baasha son of Ahijah.
And the dogs will eat Jezebel in the plot of ground at Jezreel, and no one will bury her.’” Then the young prophet opened the door and fled.
When Jehu came out to the servants of his master, they asked, “Is everything all right? Why did this madman come to you?” He answered, “You know the man and his babble.”
They said, “A lie! Tell us now.” So he said, “He spoke to me about this and that, saying, ‘Thus says the LORD: I anoint you king over Israel.’”
Then each man quickly took his garment and put it under Jehu on the bare steps; they blew the ram’s horn and proclaimed, “Jehu is king!”
So Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi conspired against Joram. Now Joram and all Israel had been defending Ramoth-gilead against Hazael king of Aram,

but King Joram had returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds the Arameans had given him in the battle against Hazael their king. Jehu said, “If this is your wish to make me king, let no one slip out of the city to go tell it in Jezreel.”
Then Jehu got into his chariot and went to Jezreel, for Joram was lying there; and Ahaziah king of Judah had gone down to visit him.
The watchman standing on the tower in Jezreel saw Jehu’s company coming and called out, “I see a company!” Joram said, “Choose a horseman and send him to meet them, and let him ask, ‘Is it peace?’”

So a horseman rode to meet Jehu and said, “Thus says the king: ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu said, “What do you have to do with peace? Fall in behind me.” And the watchman reported, “The messenger reached them but is not coming back.”
So the king sent a second horseman, who came to them and said, “Thus says the king: ‘Is it peace?’” Jehu said, “What do you have to do with peace? Fall in behind me.”
The watchman reported again, “He reached them but is not coming back. And the driving is like the driving of Jehu son of Nimshi, for he drives like a madman.”
Joram said, “Harness!” and they harnessed his chariot. Then Joram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went out, each in his chariot, and met Jehu on the plot of Naboth the Jezreelite.
When Joram saw Jehu, he asked, “Is it peace, Jehu?” He answered, “What peace, as long as the idolatry and witchcraft of your mother Jezebel are so many?”
Joram turned and fled, calling to Ahaziah, “Treachery, Ahaziah!”
Then Jehu drew his bow and shot Joram between the shoulders; the arrow pierced his heart, and he slumped down in his chariot.

Jehu said to Bidkar his officer, “Pick him up and throw him into the plot of Naboth the Jezreelite. Remember that when you and I rode together behind Ahab his father, the LORD pronounced this prophecy against him:

‘As surely as I saw the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons yesterday, declares the LORD, so will I repay you on this plot, declares the LORD.’ Now then, pick him up and throw him onto the plot, according to the word of the LORD.”

When King Ahaziah of Judah saw this, he fled by the way of Beth-haggan. Jehu pursued him, shouting, “Shoot him too!” So they shot Ahaziah in his chariot at the Ascent of Gur near Ibleam, and he fled to Megiddo and died there.
His servants carried him by chariot to Jerusalem and buried him with his fathers in his tomb in the City of David.
(Ahaziah had become king over Judah in the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab.)
When Jehu came to Jezreel, Jezebel heard of it; she painted her eyes, adorned her head, and looked down from a window.
As Jehu entered the gate, she asked, “Is it peace, Zimri, murderer of his master?”
He looked up at the window and called, “Who is on my side? Who?” And two or three eunuchs looked down at him.
He said, “Throw her down!” So they threw her down, and her blood spattered on the wall and on the horses, and they trampled her.
Then he went in and ate and drank, and said, “See to this cursed woman and bury her, for she was a king’s daughter.”
But when they went to bury her, they found nothing but her skull, her feet, and the palms of her hands.

So they returned and told Jehu, who said, “This is the word of the LORD that He spoke through His servant Elijah the Tishbite: ‘In the plot of ground at Jezreel the dogs will eat the flesh of Jezebel.
And the corpse of Jezebel will be like dung on the field in the plot at Jezreel, so that no one can say, This is Jezebel.’”
